channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Gauteng Municipalities Intensify Removal of Illegal Structures to Improve Safety and Order
item.description: Municipalities across Gauteng have stepped up efforts to demolish illegal informal structures and unregulated settlements, citing the need to improve safety, reduce overcrowding, and enhance municipal...

item.title: Prof Dr Sarwar Khawaja honoured with Sitara-e-Imtiaz for contributions to education and entrepreneurship
item.description: LONDON/ISLAMABAD: Prof Dr Sarwar Muhammad Khawaja, a British-Pakistani educationist and entrepreneur, has been awarded the Sitara-e-Imtiaz, one of Pakistan’s highest civilian awards, by President Asif...

item.title: Trump visits China amid global tensions and domestic policy updates
item.description: On May 14, 2026, President Donald Trump arrived in Beijing for the first state visit to China by a US leader in nine years. The summit comes as the world's two largest economies aim to stabilise their...

item.title: Severe storm kills 33 and damages property across five districts of Uttar Pradesh
item.description: On Wednesday, five districts in Uttar Pradesh experienced a severe storm accompanied by heavy rain, resulting in the deaths of at least 33 people, officials reported to PTI. Bhadohi recorded the highe...

item.title: Missouri Supreme Court ruling could allow Democrats to challenge GOP redistricting map
item.description: Missouri's Supreme Court recently upheld the Republican Party's new congressional map, which heavily favors GOP candidates with a 7-1 seat advantage in the House of Representatives. This map could thr...

item.title: Tanzania Police arrest 39 in prostitution crackdown and confiscate illegal substances
item.description: Tanzania Police recently conducted a major operation targeting illegal prostitution activities across the country, leading to the arrest of 39 individuals. During the crackdown, authorities also seize...

item.title: USDA Secretary Brooke Rollins Faces Lawsuit Over Religious Messages in Workplace
item.description: Brooke Rollins, the U.S. Secretary of Agriculture, is facing a lawsuit filed by the National Federation of Federal Employees in California, alleging that she improperly brought religion into the feder...

item.title: Canadian Grand Prix Adjusts Schedule Amid Potential Canadiens Playoff Overlap
item.description: Organizers of the Canadian Grand Prix in Montreal are preparing for an earlier race date, now set for May 24, shifted from mid-June to streamline North American travel and reduce environmental impact....

item.title: Tanzania Economic Review March 2026: Exports, Gold, Manufacturing, and Tourism See Strong Growth
item.description: During March 2026, Tanzania's economy showed positive performance across multiple sectors. Exports rose by 12.8%, largely supported by a 38.5% increase in gold exports, while the manufacturing sector ...

item.title: Israeli PM Netanyahu Made a Secret Visit to UAE During Iran Conflict
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu’s office confirmed that he made a confidential visit to the United Arab Emirates during the ongoing Iran war. During this undisclosed trip, he reportedly met ...

item.title: PSG Win Fifth Consecutive Ligue 1 Title, Extend Domestic Dominance
item.description: Paris Saint-Germain don show say dem no dey joke for Ligue 1 as dem clinch their fifth straight French league title after dem waka go Lens ground comot with 2-0 win. Khvicha Kvaratskhelia score 19th g...

item.title: Implications of the U.S. Troop Withdrawal from Germany
item.description: On May 1, 2026, President Donald Trump announced plans to withdraw 5,000 U.S. troops from Germany, who were initially deployed in response to Russia’s invasion of Ukraine. The announcement follows gro...

item.title: A Guide to the Most Notable Songs in Eurovision 2026
item.description: Eurovision 2026, hosted in Austria, promises a spectacular mix of music, theatrics and controversy as it celebrates its 70th anniversary. The event has faced political tensions, with Spain, the Nether...

item.title: Exploring the Art and Legacy of Carl Spitzweg
item.description: Carl Spitzweg (1808–1885) was a prominent German painter and illustrator during the Biedermeier period, celebrated for his meticulous attention to detail and narrative-rich genre paintings. Originally...

item.title: Screenings of Oscar-nominated film 'The Voice of Hind Rajab' begin in South Africa
item.description: The Oscar-nominated film 'The Voice of Hind Rajab' will start screening in South African cinemas this weekend, bringing international attention to the tragic final hours of five-year-old Hind Rajab du...

item.title: Family Homes Funds trains 572 artisans with job opportunities in Abuja
item.description: Family Homes Funds Limited don successfully graduate 572 artisans from im pilot skills acquisition programme. The graduation ceremony happen for Karsana, Abuja, where 72 beneficiaries based in Abuja d...

item.title: Hearts and Celtic set for decisive final-day Scottish Premiership showdown after late penalty controversy
item.description: Hearts manager Derek McInnes has condemned a last-minute penalty awarded to Celtic as 'disgusting', following a 3-0 victory over Falkirk that would have put Hearts in control of the Scottish Premiersh...

item.title: OnlyFans Creators Navigate Leaving the Platform and Managing Online Privacy
item.description: As OnlyFans experiences a wave of early creators exiting the platform, questions about consent, privacy, and the long-term impact of online sex work have come to the forefront. Creators like Win White...

item.title: Reflections on Kitty Go’s Map-Themed Art Exhibition
item.description: Kitty Go, a renowned art professional and journalist, recently showcased a unique map-themed exhibition titled 'World on a Wall.' The exhibit offered visitors a chance to explore her artistic interpre...

item.title: Metropolitan Police deploy thousands of officers to manage rival London protests
item.description: The Metropolitan Police is preparing for one of its largest protest deployments in decades, as rival demonstrations are scheduled to take place in central London this Saturday. More than 4,000 officer...
